stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of noise level using a noise meter. The measurements have a mean of 111.5 dB and a standard deviation of 13.7 dB. However, calibration tests reveal that a noise meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.3% and 5.7%. Estimate the true noise level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 111.5 dB. After correcting for the 4.0% bias: ≈ 116.1 dB. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.3%-5.7%) and sampling variability: [105.4, 126.9]. Point estimate: 116.1 dB.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
